title: Easy-read / low-literacy guide variants
context: oer-science-labs targets educators with no laboratory, no budget, and limited connectivity — including those working with learners who have limited literacy or who need simplified language. Standard guides are written for a general grade-band reading level, but some learners (younger children, ELL students, learners with cognitive disabilities, or those in low-literacy settings) benefit from alternative versions with simplified vocabulary, shorter sentences, more visuals, and enhanced accessibility. These variants extend the library's reach without replacing the peer-reviewed originals.
objective: Produce easy-read / low-literacy variants of a representative set of published guides. Each variant must retain the full safety block (no simplification that removes a hazard warning, PPE requirement, minAge, supervision level, allergen/ingestion note, disposal instruction, or prohibited substitution) and must be reviewed for both accessibility/readability and science/safety accuracy. Variants are distinct documents linked to their parent guide and carry the same outputLicense (CC-BY-4.0).
acceptanceCriteria: Easy-read variants are produced for at least 3 published guides, chosen to span at least 2 grade bands

Each variant uses simplified vocabulary and shorter sentences appropriate for a lower reading level, verified by a readability check tool (target: Flesch-Kincaid or equivalent grade level below the standard version)

Every safety callout from the parent guide is preserved in the variant — no hazard warning, PPE requirement, minAge, supervision level, allergen/ingestion note, disposal instruction, or prohibited substitution is omitted or softened

Each variant is reviewed for accessibility and readability by an a11y reviewer or low-literacy specialist (distinct from the author) and for science/safety accuracy by reviewers distinct from the author
Variants are linked to their parent guide in the schema and clearly labeled as simplified versions; the parent guide's review log is referenced
Variants conform to the guide schema (data-003), pass CI schema-validation and the exclusion check, and carry outputLicense: CC-BY-4.0 with source attribution preserved
The authoring process for easy-read variants is documented so future contributors can produce additional variants consistently
output: A pull request adding easy-read / low-literacy variants of at least 3 published guides (with full safety blocks preserved, accessibility/readability reviewed, and science/safety accuracy reviewed) to the oer-science-labs repository.
